name: Agent4Edu Real Learner Response Comparison Extract
population_or_context: Personalized learning and computerized adaptive testing simulator for Chinese high school mathematics and physics response records.
sample_size: EduData comprises 18,045 time-ordered response records from 500 Chinese high school students.
methods: Extracted the real-response comparator setup for evaluating generated learner responses against human learner response records.
education_context: Personalized learning and computerized adaptive testing contexts with time-ordered learner response data.
response_origin: LLM-powered generative agents produce learner response data initialized from real-world learner profiles and histories.
rubric_label_space: Practice-response outcomes and learner behavior signals rather than a single analytic scoring rubric.
label_source: Real-world learner response data initializes profiles and provides the human-response comparator; generated responses come from the agent action module.
generation_model: GPT-3.5-turbo-1106 and GPT-4-turbo are used in the experiments; unless otherwise specified, the paper reports GPT-3.5-turbo as the default.
generation_method: Generative agents equipped with learner profile, memory, reflection, and action modules generate response data while interacting with personalized learning algorithms.
prompt_strategy: Agent state and module design condition response generation rather than a single one-shot prompt.
evaluation_method: Generated learner responses are evaluated with consistency and discrepancy metrics against human learner responses, including ACC, F1-score, and ROUGE-3.
scorer_use: Simulation and algorithm evaluation for personalized learning systems; not direct automated scorer validation.
real_response_comparator: Human learner responses from EduData serve as the comparator for generated response consistency.
reported_metrics: Agent4Edu reports 66.70 ACC, 79.84 F1-score, and 37.97 ROUGE-3 for the GPT-3.5-turbo condition in Table 1.
limitations: The comparator is a personalized learning response-record dataset, not a rubric-scored open-response dataset.
The source evaluates simulator fidelity and downstream personalized-learning use, not automated scorer validity.
The generated responses are initialized from real learner histories, so independence from the real comparator must be tracked.
